title: Number
description: Number provides numeric text entry with min, max, and step constraints on top of the shared field system, making it suitable for quantities, pricing, bounded values, and small calculation flows.
intro: Use `ui-number` for quantities, currency-like inputs, percentages, rates, and other numeric values that benefit from browser-native number input behavior plus your shared field styling and validation patterns.

featureSections.title: Basic numeric inputs
featureSections.description: The simplest use cases are counts and decimal values. This is the right baseline for seats, quantities, totals, or pricing fields.

featureSections.title: Min, max, and step ranges
featureSections.description: Range constraints and step increments are the most important behavior differences between a plain text field and a dedicated number field.

featureSections.title: Size and input variant
featureSections.description: Size and visual variant help the same numeric field fit dense tables, standard forms, or more prominent pricing and configuration surfaces.

featureSections.title: States and validation
featureSections.description: Readonly, disabled, required, and error states matter just as much as numeric constraints when the field participates in real forms.

featureSections.title: Budget or estimate form
featureSections.description: A realistic numeric form usually combines multiple fields that contribute to a derived total, estimate, or threshold rather than standing alone.

accessibility.title: Label and description
accessibility.body: `ui-number` inherits the shared field labeling model. Use the visible label as the primary accessible name and reserve `ariaLabel` for exceptional cases where no visible label exists.

accessibility.title: Keyboard behavior
accessibility.body:
Keyboard behavior largely follows the native number input.

| Key | Action |
| --- | --- |
| Tab / Shift+Tab | Moves focus to or from the input. |
| ArrowUp / ArrowDown | May increment or decrement the native number input depending on browser behavior. |
| Enter | Follows the surrounding form behavior rather than a custom component action. |

accessibility.title: Validation and constraints
accessibility.body: When you use `min`, `max`, and `step`, keep the visible help text or validation feedback aligned with those constraints so users understand why a value was clamped or rejected.

apiSections.title: NumberComponent - ui-number
apiSections.lead: Standalone numeric field component built on top of the shared field primitive. It adds numeric parsing plus `step`, `min`, and `max` constraints while keeping the same form and validation integration as other field components.

blocks.title: Inputs
rows: Native form and browser autofill wiring.
Numeric increment size exposed through the native `step` attribute.

blocks.title: Inherited field inputs
rows: Shared field surface token inherited from `FieldComponent`.
Controls where the field label is placed relative to the control.
Supporting helper text rendered below the field when there is no active error.
Validation or manual error message managed by the shared field base.
Disables the field and its interactive affordances.
Keeps the value visible while preventing user changes.
HTML form name used by the field wrapper and native control wiring.
Control id used to connect label, help text, and error messaging.
Inherited autocomplete hint for native form compatibility.
Inherited shared-field clear-button flag when supported by the concrete control.
Overrides the accessible name when no visible label is appropriate.
Appends extra descriptive ids to the computed `aria-describedby` value.
Enables inherited automatic validation error handling from `FieldComponent`.

blocks.title: Outputs
rows: Emits when the numeric value changes.

blocks.title: Methods
rows: Increases the current value by `step` while respecting `max` and readonly/disabled state.
Decreases the current value by `step` while respecting `min` and readonly/disabled state.
Clears the current value through the shared field behavior.

paragraphs: The component inherits its general field behavior from the shared field base, so form integration, validation, and common field styling work the same way as for text, email, or password.
Use `ui-number` only for real numeric values. If the value is an identifier such as an account number or phone-like string, a text-oriented field is usually safer.
blocks.title: Notes
